Persea shawii is classified in the kingdom Plantae, in the family Lauraceae, and in the genus Persea. The species is native to New Zealand and is also known as the New Zealand avocado or the rewarewa. It is a small to medium-sized tree with edible fruit, but not as widely cultivated as the Persea americana. Persea shawii is part of the order Laurales and is closely related to other species in the same family. The exact classification may be subject to change as new research emerges.

intro: Persea shawii, also known as the Chilean avocado or Chilean avocado tree, is a species of tree in the Lauraceae family, native to the temperate regions of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal and culinary properties, offering several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its ability to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions such as digestive issues and respiratory problems.
